title: T-015 — API: BOM version diff (GET .../boms/diff?from=&to=)
prompt:
# T-015 — API: BOM version diff (GET .../boms/diff?from=&to=)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §7.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Endpoint returning structured diff between two BOM versions.

Diff keyed by component_item_id (lines) and co_product_item_id (co_products). Header diff lists yield_pct, status, effective_from changes. Numeric diffs return delta and percent change.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/boms/diff/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/diff.ts [create]
- tests/api/bom-diff.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Side-by-side renderer (UI)
- Snapshot diff

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given v1 has 3 lines and v2 adds one and changes a quantity, when GET /diff?from=1&to=2, then response contains added=[], changed=[], removed=[] with one entry each in changed and added.
2. Given v1=v2, when GET /diff, then all arrays empty.
3. Given non-existent version pair, when GET /diff, then 404.

## Test strategy
- RED tests asserting shape of changed/added/removed arrays.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/bom-diff.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not include private_jsonb in diff.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
